exemplar.title: "Modelling the function of a power line"

detailedExemplarData.title: "Modelling the function of a power line"

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear macro-structure with descriptive headings and logical progression from aim through proof to conclusion
Consistent use of embedded figures close to relevant text aiding coherence
Title and introduction clearly present the focus of the exploration
weaknesses: Occasional digressions (long quotations, repeated screenshots) interrupt the flow
Some algebraic sections are over-detailed and could be summarised
Duplicated figures and mislabelled captions reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands were assessed at band 3 (coherence and logical development; organization and structure) and one at band 2 (conciseness). The overall logical progression is sound and the structure clear, but intermittent redundancies and labeling errors prevent an exemplary score. A score of 3 best reflects this performance.

strengths: Mostly consistent and correct use of mathematical notation (e.g. cosh, sinh, integrals)
Effective integration of hand sketches, Desmos plots and spreadsheet graphs
Logical flow in explanations with conclusions drawn at appropriate points
weaknesses: Minor notation inconsistencies ("log" vs âlg", bolding of vectors)
Some paragraphs are overly wordy or contain typos
Key steps (e.g. u-substitution in â«secÎ¸) are sketched too rapidly without full justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands were judged at band 3. The student demonstrates effective mathematical communication with appropriate notation and multiple representations, but minor slips and occasional lack of detail prevent a top-band score.

strengths: Clear personal context (near-home power line) that motivates the investigation
Good initiative in using Maclaurin-series length matching to explore the model
Evidence of independent thought in deriving the catenary from first principles
weaknesses: Approach follows conventional methods without exceptional innovation
Acknowledged use of AI prompts questions about originality
Lacks deeper personal reflection or anecdotal insight into why the context matters
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand was placed in band 2. The exploration shows clear personal engagement and some creativity, but it does not rise to the level of significant innovation or outstanding independence required for band 3.

strengths: Thoughtful analysis of model sensitivity (how varying âaâ affects cable shape)
Clear evaluation of outcomes against real-world measurements and identification of main assumptions
Insightful observation of implications (e.g. small length changes causing large sag)
weaknesses: Reflection remains mainly descriptive and stops short of probing broader modelling alternatives
Critical analysis of errors in the proof is limited
No comprehensive plan for future refinements or additional variables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All reflection strands were assessed at band 2: meaningful reflection is present, with clear evaluation, but it lacks the depth and critical insight of band 3. A score of 2 accurately represents this level.

strengths: Relevant HL-level mathematics (ODEs, arc-length calculus, hyperbolic functions) applied appropriately
Accurate separation of variables and integration steps leading to the catenary model
Correct use of Maclaurin series for approximation and handling of boundary conditions
weaknesses: Conceptual inaccuracies in integral differentiation and mis-labelled density notation
Errors in partial-fraction constants and lack of justification for some algebraic steps
Arbitrary assumption of cable length without sensitivity exploration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands were rated at band 3 for relevance and band 4 for level-appropriateness and understanding. The mathematics is fully commensurate with course expectations and largely correct, but notable slips and missing justifications prevent reaching the highest bands. A score of 4 is therefore appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations normally precede the algebra they justify and conclusions are drawn at appropriate points, giving clear communication.  Some paragraphs are wordy or contain typos, so clarity is good rather than excellent.

Derivations generally follow correct lines, but there are conceptual inaccuracies (treating arc-length integral differentiation casually, mis-labelled density symbols) and the final model is numerically inconsistent.  Understanding is good yet not thorough or error-free.

The student supplies algebraic manipulation, hand sketches, Desmos plots and a spreadsheet graph.  These representations are relevant and generally integrated with the discussion, demonstrating effective (though not exemplary) use of multiple forms.

The investigation progresses from a clearly-stated aim through proof, parameter determination and a final evaluation.  Although some algebraic sections are over-detailed, the reader can follow the argument without having to reconstruct missing steps.  Occasional digressions (long quotations, repeated screen-shots) interrupt the flow, so the development is not exemplary, but overall logic is sound and sequential.

Using a Maclaurin-series length-matching technique and plotting ânumber of terms vs lengthâ shows creativity, but the idea is executed in a straightforward way and relies heavily on calculators; the level of initiative is good, not exceptional.

The student analyses the sensitivity of âaâ and discusses the hazard of cable sag, demonstrating thoughtful but limited critical analysis.  Errors in the proof are not scrutinised in depth, so insight is moderate.

Outcomes are evaluated against reality, and potential improvements (better length measurement) are suggested.  Evaluation is clear but lacks a comprehensive plan for refinement or future work, keeping it at a meaningful rather than substantial level.

The conclusion reflects on why the model fails and how length assumptions affect sag.  Reflection is present throughout but remains mainly descriptive; it stops short of probing broader implications or alternative modelling strategies.

Standard symbols (\cosh, \sinh, \int) are used correctly and variables are defined.  Minor slips such as âlogâ vs â\lgâ and inconsistent bolding occur, preventing full accuracy, yet the language is mostly appropriate.

Personal context (near-home power line) and the decision to assume data for simplification indicate a clear personal approach.  However, once the set-up is stated, the work proceeds in a conventional manner without notable innovation.

At 20 pages the write-up contains lengthy algebra that could be summarised, repeated calculator screenshots and several explanatory sentences that do not advance the mathematics.  Most material is relevant but there is noticeable redundancy; conciseness is therefore only partial.

Hyperbolic functions, calculus of arc length, differential equations and Maclaurin series are all relevant to HL and applied appropriately to the modelling goal.  Despite some algebraic slips, the mathematical content is undeniably pertinent and substantial.

Deriving the catenary from first principles and choosing a local power line show genuine initiative, but the approach follows textbook methods and assistance from online AI is acknowledged.  Independent thought is evident yet not outstanding.

Headings (Introduction, Proof, Variables, Conclusion) give a clear macro-structure, and sub-sections are largely in a sensible order.  Figures are embedded close to the text they support.  However, some figures are duplicated and captions occasionally mislabeled, so the structure is good rather than outstanding.

The mathematics used (second-order ODE, series approximation) is fully commensurate with HL expectations and applied in context.  The work does not push beyond syllabus level into advanced university methods, hence the âfully commensurateâ rather than âexpertâ band.

annotations.comment: The historical reference to Galileo and the catenary relies on a single Wikipedia citation; a more authoritative source or multiple references would strengthen credibility.
The notation âlogâ in the vertical force vector is unclear; it should explicitly state massÃgravity (e.g. ÏÂ·gÂ·l). Correct this to avoid confusion.
When splitting vectors, maintain consistent notation (e.g. bold for vectors, correct arrow notation) and clearly define each component before using them.
The statement that differentiating both sides âcancels out the integralâ lacks justification. Include the fundamental theorem of calculus or clarify each differentiation step.
The title is clearly presented and immediately informs the reader of the investigationâs focus, demonstrating strong coherence and engagement.
The choice of a nearby power line shows personal interest, but consider adding a brief anecdote or reflection to illustrate why this context is meaningful to you.
Assuming the cable length as 40 m without sensitivity analysis is arbitrary; consider exploring how different assumed lengths affect your modelâs parameters.
The hand-drawn force diagram (Figure 1) is well integrated and helps clarify the vector components, effectively supporting your explanation.
The partial fractions values for A and B appear to be deduced incorrectly; re-solve A(1âu)+B(1+u)=1 carefully to verify constants.
The use of initial conditions to determine D is succinct and correctly eliminates the constant, showcasing precise handling of boundary values.
Introduce the roles of b and c explicitly before presenting y=1/a cosh(a(x+b))+c so readers understand the modelâs degrees of freedom.
The description of term cancellation in the two Maclaurin series is concise, but expand on why odd terms cancel and even terms double for full clarity.
The integration of â«sec Î¸ is sketched rapidly; include the u-substitution and the identity dÎ¸=[â¦] to improve clarity and traceability.
The algebraic steps leading to w=Â½(e^{ax}âe^{âax}) are concise and accurate, reflecting strong command of exponent manipulation.
The separation of variables and integration to obtain y is well structured, with each integral clearly identified and resolved.
Recognizing (e^x+e^{âx})/2 as cosh x is an excellent synthesis of hyperbolic function theory, confirming the catenary form.
Justify the temporary removal of the horizontal shift b in the algebraic solving process to maintain mathematical rigor and clarity.
The Maclaurin series for e^x is cited correctly from the formula booklet, establishing a solid foundation for approximation.
You mention using AI (ChatGPT) to compute a critical parameter; reflect explicitly on how this affects the originality and independence of your work.
The frequent calculator and screenshot inserts disrupt conciseness; summarise computational results in context and reserve graphics for critical data.
The exploration of how varying âaâ influences the cableâs shape via graphs demonstrates strong critical evaluation of model limitations.
Relying on ChatGPT for the value of a is pragmatic, but consider critically evaluating its output and discussing any discrepancies you observed.
The conclusion clearly identifies the primary assumption (cable length) and its impact on the model outcome, showing reflective depth.
Noting the real-world implication that a 2 cm length change leads to a 50 cm sag shows insightful understanding of model sensitivity.
Consider extending your reflection by discussing additional real-world variables (e.g. pole tilt, wind load) and how they might alter the catenary model.
